behaviors.description: Primary translator output is grammatically coherent and meaning-preserving (not literal word substitution).
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real neural translation quality (coherence + meaning preservation), which a stub cannot validate. MOCK-HIDE: mocked translator outputs would always hide true model-quality failures. MULTI-STEP: only one model decision is required, so Workflow is disqualified. REJECTION-GATE: System is disqualified because behavior text is about output quality, not infra behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real translation model on a multilingual evaluation set (idioms, tense, syntax-sensitive sentences) and assert quality thresholds for meaning preservation and grammaticality; fail if output regresses to literal/incorrect translations.

behaviors.description: On API 503, service transparently falls back to dictionary translation and does not return an error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (API client -> fallback path -> response handling). REAL-INFRA: real network is not required; an in-process fake 503 response reproduces the failure. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess, (b) no dependence on model-output quality, (c) no multi-agentic chain. REJECTION-GATE: System is disqualified by behavior text focusing on 503 handling logic, not transport-level conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service + API client stub + SQLite-backed fallback; force API stub to return 503 and assert caller receives fallback translation with success status (no propagated error).

behaviors.description: Dictionary fallback returns the correct stored translation for phrases present in SQLite.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets SQLite-backed lookup, so the failure boundary is module+DB interaction, not pure function logic. REAL-INFRA: local DB wiring is needed, but not OS/network/subprocess complexity beyond Integration scope. REJECTION-GATE: Unit is disqualified because behavior text requires validating real SQLite retrieval, not just in-memory mapping.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a test SQLite DB with known phrase/translation rows, invoke fallback translator, and assert exact stored translation is returned for existing phrases.

behaviors.description: Quality tracking computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: The behavior combines algorithm correctness with persistence side effects ('and stores the metric'), so cross-module verification is needed. A pure Unit test would miss write-path/schema/transaction wiring failures. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real network/subprocess needed; no AI-output-quality dependence; no multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run comparison pipeline with fixed primary/fallback strings, assert computed character-level edit distance matches expected value, and verify metric row is persisted correctly in storage.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, service returns primary translation and logs quality comparison in background.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode is orchestration/state handoff: selection priority (primary wins) plus asynchronous metric logging. This is module interaction, not model-quality evaluation. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real network/OS primitive if both translators are test doubles/local modules, (b) correctness does not depend on semantic quality of model output, (c) not a multi-agentic chain. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic is disqualified because behavior text checks routing/background side effect, not whether model translation is good.
behaviors.test_description: With both translators enabled (deterministic outputs), assert immediate response equals primary output, then assert background task records a quality comparison entry without blocking the response path.
